title: First-year writing assignment AI-use rule
purpose: Make one assignment-level AI-use boundary clear before learners start work.
review_cadence: Before each assignment cycle or when institution policy changes.

allowed_uses.use: Ask AI to generate possible research questions before choosing one
Ask for grammar or clarity feedback after writing a draft
Ask for a checklist of source-citation items to verify manually

disallowed_uses.use: Submit AI-generated paragraphs as original work
disallowed_uses.reason: Final wording and reasoning must remain learner-owned

disallowed_uses.use: Invent or accept citations without checking the source
disallowed_uses.reason: Citation trails must be manually verifiable

disallowed_uses.use: Use AI to avoid reading assigned sources or explaining reasoning in your own words
disallowed_uses.reason: The assignment assesses source interpretation and explanation

required_evidence.evidence: One short disclosure note describing any AI help used
Source notes showing which claims came from which assigned source
A visible revision note explaining what changed after feedback

human_review_triggers.trigger: Student cannot explain a claim in their own words
Citation trail is missing or source cannot be found
AI use falls outside the allowed list

sections.heading: Task context
sections.body: Learners may use AI for brainstorming questions and checking grammar after drafting, but the thesis, evidence selection, source interpretation, and final wording remain learner-owned.

sections.heading: Allowed uses
items: Ask AI to generate possible research questions before choosing one
Ask for grammar or clarity feedback after writing a draft
Ask for a checklist of source-citation items to verify manually

sections.heading: Disallowed uses
items: Submit AI-generated paragraphs as original work
Invent or accept citations without checking the source
Use AI to avoid reading assigned sources or explaining reasoning in your own words

sections.heading: Required evidence
items: One short disclosure note describing any AI help used
Source notes showing which claims came from which assigned source
A visible revision note explaining what changed after feedback

sections.heading: Human review trigger
items: Student cannot explain a claim in their own words
Citation trail is missing or source cannot be found
AI use falls outside the allowed list

review_flags: Requires local policy-owner/instructor approval before classroom use
boundary_note: Review aid only. Local school, instructor, or workplace policy controls final allowed use. Do not use this card as legal, academic-integrity, or compliance advice.
notes: Synthetic example designed to avoid shaming informal AI use while making boundaries concrete.
